excerpt: Camp gives a Huế population baseline of about 140,000 for the battle window.
confidence_note: Useful baseline for the civilian denominator, but not a direct exposure measure once displacement begins.

excerpt: Camp reports more than 116,000 homeless by the end of the battle.
transform_note: Homelessness is not identical to leaving the exposed area; used as displacement severity evidence, not a direct subtraction.
confidence_note: Important denominator constraint but not a clean 'outside theater' count.

excerpt: Camp's top-line civilian devastation figure is 5,800 residents reported dead or missing.
transform_note: Used only as an upper-bound context claim because it mixes confirmed deaths with unresolved missing persons.
confidence_note: High salience but not a clean direct-death numerator.

excerpt: By the second week of February, Villard reports about 5,000 refugees at the main Catholic church.
transform_note: Supports in-theater displacement rather than out-of-theater evacuation.
confidence_note: Useful lower-bound indicator of large civilian concentration at refuge sites.

excerpt: Villard records roughly 17,000 refugees at Hue University by the second week.
transform_note: Supports in-theater concentration of displaced civilians.
confidence_note: Indicative of severe but not necessarily external displacement.

excerpt: Another 40,000 civilians are described as displaced in the Citadel sector.
transform_note: Treated as evidence that many displaced civilians remained within the broader Huế exposure footprint.
confidence_note: Important for avoiding an excessive denominator collapse.

excerpt: Camp shows the pre-assault South Vietnamese garrison inside greater Huế was under 1,000 troops.
transform_note: Upper-bound only; used to anchor the battle's low initial allied force presence before reinforcements.
confidence_note: Important for force-presence chronology but not a full-episode exposure measure.

excerpt: Villard describes the U.S. military presence in Huế as only about 200 troops at any given time.
transform_note: Represented as a narrow band around the source's approximate wording for later modeling.
confidence_note: Useful outbreak anchor only; reinforcements quickly changed the exposure picture.

excerpt: Camp estimates 8,000-11,000 enemy participants in the city or its approaches.
confidence_note: Primary source-based anchor for enemy force exposure.

excerpt: Summing Camp's component figures gives 637 allied military dead in the episode.
transform_note: Derived from component figures reported in the same source: Marines 142, ARVN 333, Vietnamese Marines 88, 1st Cavalry 68, 101st Airborne 6.
confidence_note: Strongest component of the military numerator.

excerpt: Camp cites captured Communist documents showing 1,042 troops killed.
transform_note: Treated as a floor rather than a full-episode total because the document may not cover all enemy units.
confidence_note: Likely incomplete but valuable as a lower bound.

excerpt: Camp gives the commonly cited enemy dead range as 2,500 to 5,000.
transform_note: Used as the main official KIA range but treated cautiously because body-count inflation is possible.
confidence_note: Upper bound is particularly uncertain.

excerpt: Porter says the initial provincial Social Services estimate recorded 3,776 civilians killed in the fighting.
transform_note: Used as a lower-bound direct-civilian-death interpretation because it appears to cover civilians killed in battle but may omit later execution/missing deaths.
confidence_note: Important counterweight to later political-warfare reductions and to the 'dead or missing' ceiling.

excerpt: Pike's recapitulation begins from a total of 7,600 civilian casualties resulting from the battle.
transform_note: Not ingested into the core death path because Pike's total mixes deaths, injuries, and unresolved missing-person accounting.
confidence_note: Auxiliary context only.

excerpt: Pike reports 1,900 civilians hospitalized with war wounds in March.
transform_note: Hospitalized war-wound injuries only; probably an undercount of all injuries.
confidence_note: Useful auxiliary injury series.

victim_side: South Vietnamese officials/administrative personnel and associated targets
excerpt: A captured document cited by Villard reports the elimination of nearly 3,000 'tyrants and puppet administrative personnel.'
transform_note: Excluded from the core civilian path because the victim class is mixed and includes administrative/security personnel rather than clearly civilians only.
confidence_note: Important side-attribution context for direct killings by Communist forces.

excerpt: Villard mentions at least thirty civilians apparently murdered by retreating Communist forces.
transform_note: Minimum documented side-attributed civilian direct deaths; too small to stand in for the whole civilian numerator.
confidence_note: Confirms at least some side-attributed direct civilian killing.

excerpt: Camp says South Vietnamese authorities later exhumed roughly 3,000 bodies from hasty graves.
transform_note: Excluded from clean civilian/military totals because the victim-class mix is unresolved.
confidence_note: Important context for the battle's direct-killing dimension but not a status-clean series.

victim_side: Mixed victims from Da Mai list
excerpt: Pike's 428-name Da Mai list explicitly includes 25% military, with the remainder students, civil servants, service personnel, and workers.
transform_note: This mixed-status evidence is the main reason Pike's grave-find totals are not used as a clean civilian numerator.
confidence_note: Methodologically critical exclusion rule.

excerpt: The Army campaign summary says eight enemy battalions fought against three U.S. Marine, three U.S. Army, and eleven South Vietnamese battalions at Huế.
transform_note: Used as a force-presence cross-check when modeling allied combatant exposure.
confidence_note: Not a troop count, but a useful sanity check that exposure far exceeded the small opening garrison.
